quick_facts.label: Core role
quick_facts.value: An Ayurvedic Medical Officer is a government-employed Ayurveda physician role centred on patient treatment and community-facing public-health work; official Odisha departmental material lists treatment of patients, health camps and health-awareness programmes among Ayurvedic/Unani Medical Officers’ duties.

quick_facts.label: Usual qualifying degree
quick_facts.value: BAMS is the foundational qualification route. NCISM’s 2022 BAMS regulations specify a five-and-a-half-year programme comprising three 18-month professional phases and a 12-month compulsory rotatory internship.

quick_facts.label: Registration
quick_facts.value: Registration is commonly required in recruitment: a Goa State Health Society notice for an AYUSH Doctor required a recognised Ayurveda degree and registration with the Goa board/central council of Indian medicine.

sections.heading: What an Ayurvedic Medical Officer does
paragraphs: The role combines Ayurvedic clinical service with public-health delivery. An official Odisha RTI manual assigns Ayurvedic/Unani Medical Officers responsibility for treating patients and conducting health camps and health-awareness programmes. In practice, the precise clinical scope, reporting requirements, medicine-management work and outreach duties are set by the employing state department and the specific facility.

Government AYUSH services also involve service delivery beyond an individual dispensary. Ministry of Ayush material describes National AYUSH Mission support for access to AYUSH services and for hospital/dispensary infrastructure, while state governments remain responsible for public-health service delivery and workforce deployment.
bullets: Provide Ayurveda-based outpatient clinical care within the post’s authorised service setting.
Participate in health camps and public-health awareness activities where assigned.
Work within state or UT administrative, reporting and facility procedures; the exact assignment can vary by posting.

sections.heading: Entry route after BAMS
paragraphs: The standard route begins with completing BAMS, including its compulsory rotatory internship. NCISM’s BAMS regulations set the programme duration at five years and six months, including a 12-month internship.

Candidates then need to meet the eligibility terms of a particular employer’s vacancy. These commonly include a recognised Ayurveda degree and professional registration; for example, Goa’s State Health Society required BAMS and a registration certificate for an AYUSH Doctor post. Recruitment may be through a state public service commission or through a state/district AYUSH or health society on a contractual basis. A 2026 HPPSC advertisement illustrates the commission route for an Ayurvedic Medical Officer post, while Goa’s 2026 National AYUSH Mission notice illustrates a fixed-term contractual engagement.
bullets: Complete BAMS and the compulsory internship.
Obtain the registration required by the relevant state or recruiting authority.
Track official state PSC, AYUSH department, health department, NHM and State AYUSH Society notices.
Check each notification for domicile, age, category, language, experience, examination and document-verification conditions; these are vacancy-specific.

sections.heading: Skills that fit the post
paragraphs: The clearest job-specific skills are those directly reflected in government duty descriptions: clinical patient management, communication for health-awareness work, and the ability to organise or participate in outreach camps. Because the role is delivered through a public facility, candidates should also be prepared for administrative instructions and reporting associated with their assigned dispensary or health programme.

Some vacancies add locally relevant capabilities. For example, a 2026 Goa National AYUSH Mission Medical Officer (Ayurveda) vacancy identified clinical Panchakarma or Ayurvedic-clinic/government-hospital experience as essential for that particular post, with computer knowledge and travel across the state listed as desirable or required conditions. These are examples, not universal eligibility rules.
bullets: Ayurvedic clinical assessment and patient communication.
Facility documentation, coordination and compliance with departmental instructions.
Adaptability to the specific programme or clinical focus named in the recruitment notice.

sections.heading: Where the role is performed
paragraphs: Workplaces include government Ayurvedic dispensaries and hospitals, primary-health-centre-linked services, AYUSH Health and Wellness Centres, and integrated AYUSH hospitals. The Ministry of Ayush states that the National AYUSH Mission includes provisions for contractual manpower in upgraded standalone government AYUSH hospitals, AYUSH Health and Wellness Centres, and 10-, 30- and 50-bedded integrated AYUSH hospitals.

Posting conditions can include rural or district-level service and mobility. For example, the Goa State AYUSH Society’s January 2026 Medical Officer (Ayurveda) notice placed the role at IPHB/PHC Chimbel and stated that the appointee would have to travel extensively within the state.
bullets: Integrated AYUSH facilities under state or National AYUSH Mission implementation.
Community outreach locations for camps and awareness programmes.

sections.heading: Compensation context: highly appointment-specific
paragraphs: Compensation should be read from the individual notification rather than treated as a national salary benchmark. As dated examples, HPPSC advertised an Ayurvedic Medical Officer (Job Trainee) role in February 2026 at ₹29,500 per month, while the Goa State AYUSH Society advertised a one-year National AYUSH Mission Medical Officer (Ayurveda) contract in January 2026 at ₹40,000 per month, described as ₹25,000 fixed plus ₹15,000 performance-linked incentive.

These figures are not directly comparable or predictive: the HP post was a state job-trainee appointment, whereas the Goa post was a specified fixed-term NAM contract with role-specific clinical-experience requirements. Pay, allowances, tenure, pension/benefits and progression depend on the state’s service rules and the vacancy’s employment terms.
bullets: Verify the appointment type first: regular service, job trainee, contractual/NAM or another engagement category.
Use the latest official advertisement for the stated monthly pay, allowances, tenure and benefits.
Do not extrapolate a single state’s advertised figure to all Ayurvedic Medical Officer jobs.
